title: Cylindropuntia shaferi
taxonomy: Cylindropuntia shaferi is a species of cholla cactus. It belongs to the family Cactaceae. Cylindropuntia shaferi is further classified within the subfamily Opuntioideae and the tribe Opuntieae. The species is native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the states of Arizona and New Mexico. Cylindropuntia shaferi is a type of flowering plant that produces small white flowers.

intro: Cylindropuntia shaferi, also known as Shafer's cholla, is a species of cactus in the prickly pear family (Cactaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Cylindropuntia shaferi include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations, wounds, and digestive problems.
